title: pheidonocarpa corymbosa subsp. cubensis
intro: Pheidonocarpa corymbosa subsp. cubensis, also known as the Cuban poison dart plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Loganiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a tincture or infusion, and is traditionally used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
